skill.name: test-generator
skill.description: Generate unit tests based on existing code patterns and testing frameworks.
skill.summary: Generate unit tests based on existing code patterns and testing frameworks.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and dismissed as false positives. The apparent command execution is Markdown usage examples, and the weak cryptography and reconnaissance hits are ordinary prose or JavaScript test names, not executable behavior.

content.user_title: Generate Unit Tests from Existing Code
content.value_statement: Writing complete tests is slow when a project has mixed frameworks and many edge cases. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to inspect code patterns and draft focused unit tests.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes target functions, signatures, return types, dependencies, and side effects.
Identifies common test frameworks across JavaScript, Python, Go, Java, and Ruby projects.
Generates tests for happy paths, edge cases, error handling, mocks, and asynchronous behavior.
Matches existing file names, directory layout, assertion style, and setup patterns.
Encourages coverage of public methods, boundary conditions, and integration scenarios when appropriate.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automatic test runner integration.
Requires access to project files to infer real conventions accurately.
Generated tests may need review for domain-specific behavior and fixture correctness.
Coverage targets are guidance and are not measured by the skill itself.

use_cases.title: Add Tests for New Features
use_cases.description: Generate first-pass unit tests for recently added functions while matching the repository testing framework.

use_cases.title: Improve Legacy Coverage
use_cases.description: Identify important edge cases and error paths for existing code that has limited tests.

use_cases.title: Standardize Test Structure
use_cases.description: Create tests that follow existing naming, directory, assertion, and setup conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Basic Unit Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use test-generator on this file. Create unit tests for the public functions and follow the existing framework conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Cover Edge Cases
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for stronger branch coverage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use test-generator to add tests for normal inputs, empty inputs, boundary values, and error conditions in this module.

prompt_templates.title: Match Existing Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Use test-generator to inspect nearby tests first, then create tests that match naming, setup, mocks, and assertion style.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Coverage Improvements
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for broader test planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use test-generator to review this component and propose a test set for public methods, async behavior, dependencies, and integration paths.

output_examples.input: A JavaScript service with validation, async database calls, and existing Jest tests.
output_examples.output: A Jest test plan covering valid requests, invalid input, rejected database calls, mocked dependencies, and expected response shapes.

output_examples.input: A Python parser module with existing pytest fixtures.
output_examples.output: A pytest-focused test set for successful parsing, empty data, malformed records, boundary values, and reusable fixture setup.

output_examples.input: A Go package with public functions and table-driven tests.
output_examples.output: A table-driven test outline that follows Go naming conventions and covers normal cases, errors, and boundary conditions.

best_practices: Provide the target file and nearby tests so the skill can match local conventions.
Ask for edge cases and error paths when behavior is more important than raw coverage.
Review generated mocks and fixtures against real domain rules before committing tests.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for tests without sharing the code under test.
Do not accept generated assertions that only verify implementation details.
Do not use one generic test style across projects with different frameworks.

faq.question: Which languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: It gives guidance for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, Java, and Ruby test frameworks.

faq.question: Does it run the generated tests?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides test generation guidance but does not include a runner or executable script.

faq.question: Can it improve coverage?
faq.answer: It can suggest tests for uncovered paths, but a separate coverage tool must measure the result.

faq.question: Will it match my project style?
faq.answer: It is designed to inspect nearby tests and follow existing names, assertions, setup, and teardown patterns.

faq.question: Does it create integration tests?
faq.answer: It can include integration test suggestions when they fit the code and project conventions.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found no executable code, network access, environment access, or confirmed dangerous behavior.
